title: Water Level Pressure Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is not providing correct readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E1 indicates the water level pressure sensor (also called a pressure transducer or pressure switch) is malfunctioning. The control board cannot accurately determine the water level in the tub, which affects fill, wash, and drain operations. This sensor measures air pressure in a sealed chamber connected to the tub to determine water depth.

causes.cause: Pressure sensor has failed electronically
Air hose from tub to sensor is kinked, cracked, or disconnected
Air trap at the tub connection is clogged
Control board pressure input circuit has failed

symptoms: Washer will not start fill cycle
Washer runs but water level is inconsistent
Cycle does not advance past fill stage

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel. Locate the small rubber hose running from the tub to the pressure sensor on the rear wall. Check for kinks, cracks, or disconnections. Reconnect or replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Clean the air trap
fixSteps.instruction: Where the hose connects to the tub, there is usually a small pressure dome or air trap. Remove the hose and clean out any soap residue or debris from the trap.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and blow gently into the hose. You should hear the sensor click (mechanical) or measure a voltage change (electronic). No response indicates a failed sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the sensor from its mount, disconnect the hose and wiring connector. Install the new sensor and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and hose are fine, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer E1 error pressure sensor fix

faq.q: What does the pressure sensor do?
faq.a: It measures water level in the tub by sensing air pressure in a sealed tube. As water rises, it compresses air in the tube, and the sensor converts this to an electrical signal for the control board.

faq.q: Can fabric softener clog the pressure system?
faq.a: Yes. Fabric softener and detergent residue can build up in the pressure hose and air trap, leading to inaccurate readings.

faq.q: Will E1 cause flooding?
faq.a: Potentially. If the sensor cannot read water level, the washer may overfill. Most GE washers have a backup overflow protection, but it should still be repaired promptly.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board and the motor control board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: Error E2 indicates a communication failure between the main electronic control board and the motor control unit (MCU). These two boards exchange data about cycle parameters, motor speed, and status. A broken communication link prevents the washer from operating.

causes.cause: Wire harness between boards is damaged or loose
Main control board has failed
Motor control unit has failed
Power surge damaged communication circuitry

symptoms: Error appears immediately when starting a cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then reconnect. This can reset both boards and restore communication.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel. Locate the wire harness connecting the main board (usually top rear) to the MCU (usually bottom rear near the motor). Reseat all connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect harness for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wire harness for any pinched, cut, or burned wires. Pay attention to where the harness routes past sharp edges or moving parts.

fixSteps.title: Professional board diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the harness is intact, one of the two boards has likely failed. A technician can test each board to determine which needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If checking connections does not resolve E2, professional board-level diagnosis is needed.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer E2 communication error control board fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E2?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage the communication circuitry on either control board. A surge protector is recommended.

faq.q: Is E2 expensive to fix?
faq.a: If it is just a loose connection, it is free. If a board needs replacement, expect $200-$450 including parts and labor.

faq.q: Can I determine which board is bad?
faq.a: Without diagnostic equipment, it is difficult. A technician can run board-level tests to isolate the faulty board.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor (tachometer) is not providing valid speed feedback.
fullDescription: Error E3 indicates the motor speed sensor (tachometer/Hall effect sensor) attached to the drive motor is not sending proper speed signals to the motor control unit. The MCU uses this feedback to control drum speed during wash and spin cycles.

causes.cause: Speed sensor has failed
Speed sensor connector is loose or corroded
Motor rotor magnet is damaged
Motor control unit speed input has failed

symptoms: Drum does not spin or spins erratically
Washer will not enter spin cycle

fixSteps.title: Check for overload
fixSteps.instruction: Remove some items if the drum is very full. An overloaded drum can prevent the motor from reaching the target speed, causing E3.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor from the rear panel. Find the speed sensor wiring and ensure the connector is firmly seated. Clean any corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test the speed sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance. Hall effect sensors typically read 100-300 ohms. Also check that the sensor produces a voltage signal when the motor shaft is spun by hand.

fixSteps.title: Replace the speed sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the sensor from the motor housing. Install the new sensor, ensuring it is properly aligned with the motor's magnetic ring.

whenToCallPro: If the speed sensor tests fine, the motor or MCU may need professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer E3 motor speed sensor error fix

faq.q: Can overloading cause E3?
faq.a: Yes. A heavily loaded drum prevents the motor from reaching the expected speed, which the speed sensor reports as an error.

faq.q: What is the speed sensor?
faq.a: A small sensor on the motor that generates electrical pulses proportional to motor speed. The MCU uses these pulses to control and verify drum speed.

faq.q: Is E3 the same as UE (unbalanced)?
faq.a: No. E3 is specifically a speed sensor issue, while UE means the load is unbalanced. However, a severely unbalanced load can cause speed issues that appear as E3.

title: Water Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (thermistor) is reading out of range.
fullDescription: Error E4 indicates the water temperature thermistor is providing readings outside the expected range. The control board cannot properly regulate water temperature for the selected cycle. The sensor may be open, shorted, or reading inaccurately.

causes.cause: Thermistor has failed
Thermistor connector is loose or corroded
Thermistor wiring is damaged
Control board temperature input is faulty

symptoms: Water is not heated to the correct temperature
Clothes not clean on warm/hot cycles
Washer defaults to cold water only

fixSteps.title: Check thermistor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Access the thermistor (usually in the outer tub near the water inlet or heater area). Check that the connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance. At room temperature (77F/25C), it should read approximately 10-50k ohms depending on the model. Check the service manual for the exact value.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If the thermistor tests good at the sensor, check continuity of each wire back to the control board connector.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old thermistor from its housing (usually a push-in or screw mount). Install the new one and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the thermistor and wiring test fine, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer E4 temperature sensor thermistor error fix

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water if E4 appears?
faq.a: The cold cycle may work since it does not rely on heating. However, the thermistor still needs to read correctly for proper cycle control.

faq.q: How do I find the thermistor in my GE washer?
faq.a: In front-load GE washers, the thermistor is usually in the bottom of the outer tub near the heater. In top-loaders, it may be near the water inlet.

faq.q: Is the thermistor the same as a thermostat?
faq.a: No. A thermistor provides a variable resistance reading for precise temperature measurement. A thermostat is a simple on/off switch at a set temperature.

title: Motor Circuit Error
shortDescription: The main drive motor or its control circuit has a fault.
fullDescription: Error E5 indicates a problem with the drive motor or the motor control circuitry. The motor control unit has detected abnormal conditions such as overcurrent, undercurrent, or no response from the motor when it should be running.

causes.cause: Motor winding has failed (open or short)
Motor is mechanically jammed
Motor control unit has failed
Wiring between MCU and motor is damaged

symptoms: Motor buzzes but does not turn
Circuit breaker trips when washer runs

fixSteps.title: Check for drum blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and try spinning the drum by hand. It should rotate freely. If stuck, a foreign object may be jammed between the drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. A temporary overcurrent or glitch may have triggered E5. If it clears, monitor for recurrence.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor from the rear. Disconnect the motor leads and test each winding for resistance (typically 2-8 ohms). Check for ground faults between any winding and the motor frame.

fixSteps.title: Replace the motor or MCU
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor windings are bad, the motor needs replacement. If the motor tests fine, the MCU may be faulty. Both require significant disassembly.

whenToCallPro: Motor and MCU replacement are major repairs. A professional can accurately diagnose which component is faulty and ensure proper installation.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer E5 motor error fix

faq.q: Can a jammed drum cause E5?
faq.a: Yes. If the drum cannot rotate, the motor draws excessive current, which the MCU detects as E5.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the motor?
faq.a: If the washer is under 8 years old, motor replacement ($200-$500) is usually worthwhile compared to buying a new washer.

faq.q: Can E5 trip my circuit breaker?
faq.a: Yes. A shorted motor winding can draw enough current to trip the breaker. Do not continue trying to run the washer if the breaker trips.

title: Door Lock / Door Switch Error
shortDescription: The door is not properly closed, locked, or the door switch/lock assembly is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: The dE error (and related codes dL, dU, dS) indicates a problem with the washer's door system. dE means the door is not detected as closed, dL means the lock cannot engage, dU means the door has unlocked unexpectedly, and dS means the door switch is faulty. The washer requires a confirmed locked door before operation.

causes.cause: Door is not fully closed or something is caught in the seal
Door lock assembly has failed
Door switch is defective
Door hinge is bent or misaligned

symptoms: dE, dL, dU, or dS error on display
Door does not click when closing
Door lock makes clicking noise but does not engage
Cycle stops and door unlocks unexpectedly

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is caught in the door seal or preventing full closure. Small items of clothing can get caught between the door and the rubber boot seal.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the latch on the door and the strike on the washer frame. Ensure they align properly. Check for visible wear or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the door boot seal retaining spring. Access the door lock. Disconnect and test with a multimeter for continuity in both locked and unlocked positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock tests faulty, remove the two mounting screws, disconnect the connector, and install the new lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the lock and door are fine but the error persists, the control board may not be properly reading the lock signal.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer dE door lock error fix

faq.q: What do dE, dL, dU, and dS mean?
faq.a: dE = door error, dL = door lock failure, dU = door unexpectedly unlocked, dS = door switch issue. All relate to the door system.

faq.q: Can I open the door manually if it is stuck?
faq.a: Unplug the washer and wait 5 minutes. Most GE washers release the lock when power is removed. Some models have an emergency release tab.

faq.q: Why does the door unlock during a cycle?
faq.a: The dU code means the door unlocked unexpectedly, usually due to a failing lock solenoid. This is a safety concern and the lock should be replaced.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: Error UE indicates the washer's load is unevenly distributed in the drum. During the spin cycle, the washer detects excessive vibration from an unbalanced load and stops to prevent damage. This is common with single heavy items, small loads, or loads that bunch together on one side.

causes.cause: Load is unevenly distributed in the drum
Washing a single heavy item (e.g., comforter, rug)
Suspension springs or shock absorbers are worn
Washer is not level on the floor

symptoms: UE or uE error on display
Excessive vibration or banging during spin
Washer walks or moves across the floor
Clothes still very wet at end of cycle

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and redistribute clothes evenly around the drum. Untangle any bunched items. Add a few towels if washing a single heavy item.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Use a bubble level on top of the washer. Adjust the leveling feet until the washer is level in both directions. Lock the feet with the locking nuts.

fixSteps.title: Check suspension system
fixSteps.instruction: If UE occurs frequently with properly loaded cycles, check the suspension springs (top-loaders) or shock absorbers (front-loaders) for wear. Worn suspension allows excessive drum movement.

fixSteps.title: Replace worn components
fixSteps.instruction: Replace worn shock absorbers (sold in pairs) or suspension springs. For front-loaders, the shock absorbers connect between the outer tub and the base frame.

whenToCallPro: If UE occurs with every load despite proper loading and the washer is level, the suspension system likely needs professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer UE unbalanced load error fix

faq.q: Why does UE happen with small loads?
faq.a: Small loads do not distribute evenly in the drum. When spinning at high speed, even a small imbalance is amplified. Try adding a few towels to balance the load.

faq.q: Can I prevent UE when washing a comforter?
faq.a: Wash large items with a few towels or smaller items to help balance the load. Use a lower spin speed if available.

faq.q: What is the difference between UE and uE?
faq.a: On some GE models, lowercase uE means the washer is attempting to rebalance automatically, while uppercase UE means it has failed to rebalance and needs user intervention.

title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: Water is not entering the washer or is entering too slowly.
fullDescription: Error IE (Inlet Error) indicates the washer is not receiving adequate water flow during the fill cycle. The control board detects that the water level is not rising as expected. This is typically a water supply issue rather than a washer malfunction.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets are closed or not fully open
Inlet hose filter screens are clogged
Water inlet valve has failed
Water supply hoses are kinked

symptoms: No water or very little water in the drum
Washer stops at the beginning of the cycle
Hot or cold water but not both

fixSteps.title: Open supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Check both hot and cold supply valves behind the washer. Turn them fully counterclockwise to open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply. Disconnect the hoses from the washer. Remove and clean the small mesh filter screens from each inlet port. Also check the hose-end screens.

fixSteps.title: Check hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect both supply hoses for kinks, crushing, or damage. Replace if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valves
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across each valve solenoid (500-1500 ohms is normal). Replace the valve assembly if any solenoid is open.

whenToCallPro: If supply and valves are fine but IE persists, the pressure sensor or control board may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer IE inlet error not filling water fix

faq.q: Why does IE show when my water is on?
faq.a: Even with water on, clogged inlet screens can restrict flow enough to trigger IE. Clean the screens first.

faq.q: Do I need both hot and cold water connected?
faq.a: GE recommends connecting both. Some cycles mix hot and cold. If one supply is missing, the washer may not fill properly.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause IE?
faq.a: Yes. GE washers need at least 20 PSI water pressure. Test your pressure if you suspect it is low.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water from the tub within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error OE (Overflow/Outflow Error) indicates the washer is unable to drain water. The drain pump may be blocked, the drain hose may be clogged or kinked, or the pump itself may have failed. Standing water remains in the drum.

causes.cause: Drain pump filter is clogged with debris
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump motor has failed
Standpipe is clogged

symptoms: Washer does not advance to spin

fixSteps.title: Clean the pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the drain pump filter access (usually lower front behind a panel). Place towels and a shallow container. Open the filter and remove all debris, coins, lint, and small items.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks. Ensure it is not pushed more than 6 inches into the standpipe. Check the standpipe for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the pump during a drain cycle. If silent, test the pump motor resistance (5-20 ohms). Replace if the motor is open.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect hoses and wiring from the old pump. Remove mounting clips or screws. Install the new pump and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the pump and hose are clear but OE persists, the pump motor or control board may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer OE drain error not draining fix

faq.q: How do I drain the washer manually?
faq.a: Use the emergency drain hose near the pump filter, or slowly open the pump filter cap to release water into a shallow container. Have plenty of towels ready.

faq.q: What commonly clogs the pump filter?
faq.a: Coins, hairpins, buttons, small socks, and lint are the most common items. Check pockets before washing.

faq.q: Can a clogged standpipe cause OE?
faq.a: Yes. If the standpipe (wall drain) is clogged, water backs up and cannot exit. Snake the standpipe to clear it.

title: Pressure Switch Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure switch is stuck or not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error PE specifically indicates the pressure switch (distinct from the pressure sensor in E1) has mechanically failed or is stuck. The switch may be stuck in the full or empty position, preventing proper water level control.

causes.cause: Pressure switch contacts are stuck
Air hose to the switch is clogged
Pressure switch has failed completely
Wiring to the switch is damaged

symptoms: Washer will not fill at all
Water level is always too high or too low

fixSteps.title: Clean the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the pressure hose from both the switch and the tub connection. Blow through it to clear any debris. Clean the tub air trap opening.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the switch. Blow into the switch port and listen for a click. Test continuity across the switch contacts with a multimeter while applying pressure.

fixSteps.title: Check switch wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wiring from the switch to the control board for damage or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old switch from its mount, disconnect the hose and wiring. Install the new switch.

whenToCallPro: If the switch and hose are fine, the control board pressure input may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer PE pressure switch error fix

faq.q: Can PE cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes, if the switch is stuck indicating empty, the washer may overfill continuously. Shut off the water supply immediately if this happens.

faq.q: Is PE the same as E1?
faq.a: They are related but different. E1 is an electronic pressure sensor reading error, while PE is a mechanical pressure switch failure.

faq.q: Where is the pressure switch?
faq.a: Typically mounted on the rear wall of the washer near the top, connected to a small rubber hose that leads to the tub.

title: Motor Locked / Overload Error
shortDescription: The drive motor is locked, overloaded, or unable to rotate the drum.
fullDescription: Error LE (Locked Error) indicates the motor control unit has detected that the motor is drawing excessive current because it cannot rotate the drum. This is typically caused by a mechanical obstruction, overloaded drum, or failed motor bearings.

causes.cause: Drum is severely overloaded
Foreign object jammed between drum and tub
Motor bearings have seized
Drive coupler or belt has failed

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Remove about half the laundry from the drum. Overloading is the most common cause of LE. Try running the cycle with a smaller load.

fixSteps.title: Check for foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the drum slowly by hand and feel for any obstruction or grinding. Check between the drum and tub for trapped items.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drive system
fixSteps.instruction: For belt-drive models, check the belt. For direct-drive models, check the motor coupling. A broken or worn component can prevent the drum from turning.

fixSteps.title: Check motor and bearings
fixSteps.instruction: If the drum turns hard by hand even when empty, the bearings may be seizing. Listen for grinding sounds when rotating. Also test motor resistance.

whenToCallPro: If the drum is hard to turn by hand when empty, or if the motor has a burning smell, professional diagnosis is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer LE motor locked overload error fix

faq.q: How much laundry is too much?
faq.a: The drum should be no more than three-quarters full. Clothes should be loosely placed, not tightly packed.

faq.q: Can a broken motor coupling cause LE?
faq.a: A failing motor coupling can cause the motor to work harder, triggering LE. However, a completely broken coupling usually means the motor runs but the drum does not turn.

faq.q: Is LE dangerous?
faq.a: Continued operation with LE can overheat the motor and potentially damage it. Stop using the washer and resolve the issue.

title: Heating Error
shortDescription: The washer's water heating system has failed to reach the target temperature.
fullDescription: Error tE (Temperature Error) indicates the washer's internal water heater (on models equipped with one) cannot heat the water to the selected temperature within the allowed time. This can be caused by a failed heating element, thermistor, or control board.

causes.cause: Heating element has failed
Thermistor is reading incorrectly
Control board heating relay has failed
Incoming water is extremely cold

symptoms: Water does not warm up on warm/hot cycles
Cycle takes excessively long before error

fixSteps.title: Verify hot water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Run hot water at the nearest sink for 2 minutes before starting the washer. This ensures hot water is available at the washer's connection.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heater at the bottom of the outer tub. Disconnect and test resistance (20-50 ohms typical). Also check for ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance at room temperature. Compare to the service manual specification.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the heater or thermistor as needed. The heater is sealed into the tub with a compression fitting.

whenToCallPro: If the heater and thermistor test fine, the control board relay needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer tE heating error fix

faq.q: Does my GE washer have an internal heater?
faq.a: Most GE front-load washers and some higher-end top-loaders have an internal water heater. Basic top-loaders typically rely solely on the household hot water supply.

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water while tE is active?
faq.a: Cold cycles should work normally since they do not use the heater. Warm and hot settings will not heat properly.

faq.q: Why is water heating important for washing?
faq.a: Warmer water helps dissolve detergent better and is more effective at removing oils, grease, and certain stains.

title: Water Heater Overcurrent Error
shortDescription: The water heater is drawing excessive current, indicating a potential short circuit.
fullDescription: Error HE indicates the control board has detected the water heating element is drawing more current than expected, suggesting a short circuit or ground fault in the heater circuit. This is a safety protection that shuts down heating to prevent damage or fire risk.

causes.cause: Heating element has a ground fault (shorting to the tub)
Heater wiring insulation has degraded
Control board current sensing is faulty
Water intrusion into heater electrical connections

symptoms: Circuit breaker may trip during wash
Washer stops immediately when heating starts

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer immediately. If the breaker tripped, do not reset it until the issue is diagnosed. HE involves a potential electrical fault.

fixSteps.title: Test heater for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heater element. Disconnect it. Using a multimeter, test between each heater terminal and the metal tub/chassis. Any reading other than OL indicates a ground fault.

fixSteps.title: Inspect heater connections
fixSteps.instruction: Check the heater terminals and wiring for signs of moisture, corrosion, or melting. Dry and clean connections. Replace damaged wires.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the element has a ground fault, it must be replaced. Remove the old element and install a new one with proper sealing.

whenToCallPro: HE involves potential electrical hazards. If you are not comfortable testing for ground faults, call a professional.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer HE heater overcurrent ground fault fix

faq.q: Is HE dangerous?
faq.a: HE indicates a potential electrical fault which could trip breakers or in extreme cases cause overheating. Disconnect power and repair before using the washer.

faq.q: Why does my breaker trip when the washer runs?
faq.a: A ground fault in the heating element can draw enough current through the ground wire to trip a GFCI breaker. This is actually the safety system working correctly.

faq.q: Can I use cold wash while HE is active?
faq.a: The washer may still work on cold cycles, but it is best to repair the heater fault first as it represents an electrical safety issue.

title: Current Sensing Error
shortDescription: The control board's current sensing circuit has detected an abnormal condition.
fullDescription: Error CE indicates the control board's current sensing circuitry, which monitors power consumption of the motor and heater, has detected values outside normal range. This could indicate a problem with the motor, heater, or the sensing circuit itself on the control board.

causes.cause: Control board current sensor has failed
Motor or heater drawing abnormal current
Power supply voltage is unstable
Wiring issue creating intermittent shorts

symptoms: Washer stops unexpectedly during various cycle stages
Other electrical anomalies (flickering lights on panel)
Error not consistent with any particular cycle phase

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outlet voltage is stable at 120V AC. Fluctuating power can cause false current readings.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. A transient glitch in the current sensing circuit may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check motor and heater
fixSteps.instruction: Test motor and heater resistance for normal values. Also check for ground faults. Abnormal readings from either component can trigger CE.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor, heater, and power supply are fine, the control board's current sensing circuitry is likely faulty and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: CE often requires professional board-level diagnosis since the sensing circuit is integrated into the control board.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer CE current sensing error fix

faq.q: Is CE a serious error?
faq.a: CE can indicate an electrical problem. While the washer's protection circuit is working correctly by shutting down, the root cause should be diagnosed.

faq.q: Can a power strip cause CE?
faq.a: Washers should be plugged directly into a wall outlet, not a power strip or extension cord. Voltage drop across a power strip can affect current readings.

faq.q: How much does a GE washer control board cost?
faq.a: Typically $100-$250 for the part, plus $100-$150 for professional installation.

title: Overflow Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an overflow condition with water level exceeding the safe maximum.
fullDescription: Error FE indicates the water level in the tub has exceeded the maximum safe level. The overflow protection has activated and the drain pump runs continuously to remove excess water. A stuck-open inlet valve or failed pressure sensor can cause this condition.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is stuck open
Pressure sensor or switch is reading empty when tub is full
Control board is not shutting off the valve
Siphoning from drain causing water to flow backward

symptoms: Water overflows from the tub or door
Water visible above the door glass on front-loaders

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both hot and cold supply valves behind the washer immediately. Then disconnect power.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With water supply off and the tub empty, slowly turn the supply back on. If you hear water flowing into the washer when it is off, the inlet valve is stuck open and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve is not stuck, test the pressure sensor. It may be reading empty when the tub is full, causing the board to keep filling.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve is stuck open, shut off water, disconnect hoses and wiring, and replace the valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: FE can cause significant water damage. If you cannot quickly identify the cause, shut off water and call a professional.
videoSearchQuery: GE washer FE overflow error fix

faq.q: FE is causing flooding. What do I do first?
faq.a: Immediately shut off the water supply valves behind the washer. Then unplug the washer. Mop up standing water to prevent damage.

faq.q: Can a stuck inlet valve flood my house?
faq.a: Yes. If the valve is stuck open and no one is home, the washer can overflow significantly. Consider installing a leak detector near your washer.

faq.q: Should I turn off water when not using the washer?
faq.a: If FE has occurred, yes. In general, it is a good practice to turn off supply valves when leaving for extended periods.
